name: habluetooth

metadata.name: habluetooth
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# habluetooth

--- **Documentation**: https://habluetooth.readthedocs.io **Source Code**: https://github.com/bluetooth-devices/habluetooth --- High availability Bluetooth ## Installation Install this via pip (or your favourite package manager): `pip install habluetooth` ## Contributors ✨ Thanks goes to these wonderful people ([emoji key](https://allcontributors.org/docs/en/emoji-key)): This project follows the [all-contributors](https://github.com/all-contributors/all-contributors) specification. Contributions of any kind welcome! ## Credits This package was created with [Copier](https://copier.readthedocs.io/) and the [browniebroke/pypackage-template](https://github.com/browniebroke/pypackage-template) project template.

methods.name: habluetooth.scanner.HaScanner._async_stop_scanner
documentation: Stop bluetooth discovery under the lock.

Operate and automatically recover a BleakScanner.

Multiple BleakScanner can be used at the same time
if there are multiple adapters. This is only useful
if the adapters are not located physically next to each other.

Example use cases are usbip, a long extension cable, usb to bluetooth
over ethernet, usb over ethernet, etc.

modules.name: habluetooth.wrappers
documentation: Bleak wrappers for bluetooth.

functions.name: habluetooth.wrappers._rssi_sorter_with_connection_failure_penalty
documentation:
Get a sorted list of scanner, device, advertisement data.

Adjusting for previous connection failures.

When a connection fails, we want to try the next best adapter so we
apply a penalty to the RSSI value to make it less likely to be chosen
for every previous connection failure.

We use the 51% of the RSSI difference between the first and second
best adapter as the penalty. This ensures we will always try the
best adapter twice before moving on to the next best adapter since
the first failure may be a transient service resolution issue.

documentation: Set the disconnect callback.

methods.name: habluetooth.wrappers.HaBleakClientWrapper._make_disconnected_callback
documentation:
Make the disconnected callback.

https://github.com/hbldh/bleak/pull/1256
The disconnected callback needs to get the top level
BleakClientWrapper instance, not the backend instance.

The signature of the callback for the backend is:
    Callable[[], None]

To make this work we need to wrap the callback in a partial
that passes the BleakClientWrapper instance as the first
argument.

methods.name: habluetooth.wrappers.HaBleakClientWrapper.connect
documentation: Connect to the specified GATT server.

methods.name: habluetooth.wrappers.HaBleakClientWrapper._async_get_backend_for_ble_device
documentation: Get the backend for a BLEDevice.

methods.name: habluetooth.wrappers.HaBleakClientWrapper._async_get_best_available_backend_and_device
documentation:
Get a best available backend and device for the given address.

This method will return the backend with the best rssi
that has a free connection slot.

methods.name: habluetooth.wrappers.HaBleakClientWrapper.disconnect
documentation: Disconnect from the device.

Wrap the BleakClient to ensure it does not shutdown our scanner.

If an address is passed into BleakClient instead of a BLEDevice,
bleak will quietly start a new scanner under the hood to resolve
the address. This can cause a conflict with our scanner. We need
to handle translating the address to the BLEDevice in this case
to avoid the whole stack from getting stuck in an in progress state
when an integration does this.
